deep_dive.title: Score-Only Distillation for Compact Dense Retrieval
deep_dive.abstract: Large embedding models improve retrieval quality, but serving large encoders online is expensive. We study whether a compact retriever can learn teacher ranking behavior from score vectors without access to teacher hidden states. The student trains on rows built from ground-truth positives and negative candidates produced by our data generation pipeline; we evaluate student-teacher hard-negative mining separately as an extension. We use a row-centered score-vector objective, a memory-efficient implementation of uniform all-pairs PairMSE loss. On a fixed eight-task evaluation panel, our distillation protocol recovers up to 50\% of the base-to-teacher gap. The distilled 0.6B student is 4.7$\times$ faster for query encoding and 9.7$\times$ faster for document encoding than sequential online teacher fusion. External-transfer performance after distillation remains mixed, so our evidence supports compression of teacher rankings under matched retrieval protocols.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: score-only distillation from large teacher to compact 0.6B student retriever
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 从大教师模型到0.6B紧凑学生检索器的仅分数蒸馏
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 从大教师模型到0.6B紧凑学生检索器的仅分数蒸馏
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 从大教师模型到0.6B紧凑学生检索器的仅分数蒸馏
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Score-Only Distillation studies compressing large embedding models into compact retrievers via score-vector distillation without teacher hidden states. The distilled 0.6B student achieves 4.7x faster query encoding and 9.7x faster document encoding, recovering up to 50% of the base-to-teacher gap, offering a transferable distillation method for compact models.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大嵌入模型在线服务成本高的问题，研究仅利用分数向量将大教师模型蒸馏为紧凑检索器的方法，无需访问教师隐状态。蒸馏后的0.6B学生模型在查询编码上快4.7倍，文档编码快9.7倍，恢复了高达50%的性能差距。该蒸馏范式可为构建轻量安全分类器提供方法借鉴。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对大嵌入模型在线服务成本高的问题，研究仅利用分数向量将大教师模型蒸馏为紧凑检索器的方法，无需访问教师隐状态。蒸馏后的0.6B学生模型在查询编码上快4.7倍，文档编码快9.7倍，恢复了高达50%的性能差距。该蒸馏范式可为构建轻量安全分类器提供方法借鉴。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: knowledge distillation from large language models into small content moderation safety models

deep_dive.title: Silent Alarm: A J-Space Protocol for Comparing Danger Recognition Across Models and Quantization Levels
deep_dive.abstract: Jailbreak-robustness research typically evaluates safety through generated responses using an LLM-as-judge approach. Such evaluations, however, are sensitive to the benchmark's grading procedure and capture only observed behavior on a given set of attacks, without directly revealing the hidden fragility of the underlying safety mechanisms. This work proposes JADR (Jacobian Assessment of Danger Recognition), a protocol that measures a model's internal representation through Jacobian space (J-space, a recently proposed workspace of verbalizable concepts) before the first response token is generated. For every prompt and layer we record the top-k J-space tokens; these are grouped into six behavioral scenario axes and compared between a danger sample based on StrongREJECT and a safe control drawn from XSTest and OKTest. The method does not call on an external judge model: the computation runs entirely locally, on the activations of the model under evaluation, which lets us compare both different models against each other and modifications of a single model -- quantization and fine-tuning in particular -- on the same terms. The final comparison rests on the proposed SafetyAUC metric, complemented with bootstrap confidence intervals. The protocol is applied to six models (Qwen3-1.7B, Qwen3-4B, Qwen3-8B, Qwen3-Uncensored-4B, Qwen3-SafeRL-4B, Gemma 2 9B) across three weight-representation regimes -- BF16, INT8, and INT4 -- and checked against an independent behavioral evaluation with the StrongREJECT grader. The metric separates models with a strong versus a weak internal safety mechanism with statistical significance and captures substantively different effects across quantization regimes.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: evaluates safety mechanisms across quantization levels (BF16, INT8, INT4) for LLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 跨量化等级评估LLM安全机制
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 跨量化等级评估LLM安全机制
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 跨量化等级评估LLM安全机制
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes JADR, a Jacobian-space protocol to measure internal danger recognition in LLMs before response generation, revealing how quantization (BF16/INT8/INT4) affects safety mechanisms across six models with a new SafetyAUC metric.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有越狱鲁棒性研究仅通过生成响应评估安全性，无法揭示内部安全机制的隐藏脆弱性。本文提出JADR协议，通过雅可比空间在首个响应token生成前测量模型内部危险识别能力，并引入SafetyAUC指标。在六个模型及BF16、INT8、INT4三种量化等级上的实验表明，该方法能以统计显著性区分强弱安全机制并捕捉量化对安全的不同影响，为量化模型安全评估提供新工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有越狱鲁棒性研究仅通过生成响应评估安全性，无法揭示内部安全机制的隐藏脆弱性。本文提出JADR协议，通过雅可比空间在首个响应token生成前测量模型内部危险识别能力，并引入SafetyAUC指标。在六个模型及BF16、INT8、INT4三种量化等级上的实验表明，该方法能以统计显著性区分强弱安全机制并捕捉量化对安全的不同影响，为量化模型安全评估提供新工具。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: quantized low-bit safety classifier or guard model for LLM content moderation
deep_dive.quality_gate_reason_cn: 动机具体且强，使用已公开的StrongREJECT基准，但论文核心是安全评估协议而非构建轻量安全分类器，与需求部分相关

deep_dive.title: Lightweight Multi-Scale Anomaly Detection for Resource-Constrained Edge Devices
deep_dive.abstract: Time-series anomaly detection is increasingly important in IoT systems, sensor networks, and edge monitoring applications, where models must operate under strict constraints on memory, latency, and power consumption. While recent deep-learning approaches have improved detection accuracy, many remain computationally expensive and often fail to capture subtle anomalies due to limited multi-scale sensitivity. Autoencoders are widely used for anomaly detection because they reconstruct normal patterns well, leading to elevated reconstruction errors for anomalous inputs. Their simplicity and efficiency also make them suitable lightweight backbones for handling multi-scale inputs. To address these challenges, we propose a Lightweight MultiScale AutoEncoder (LMSAE) network for univariate time-series anomaly detection, designed to be compact and computationally efficient. LMSAE leverages the Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) to extract multi-scale features and employs a multi-scale loss function to improve sensitivity to subtle or hidden anomalies. Experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate competitive or superior detection performance despite using significantly fewer parameters and a model size of less than 500 KB. LMSAE also achieves low-latency, low-power inference on the NVIDIA Jetson Nano, with 9x reduction in inference latency and 2x reduction in power consumption, making it ideal for edge deployment.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Lightweight edge-deployable anomaly detection with sub-500KB model and Jetson Nano benchmarks
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 轻量端侧可部署异常检测，模型小于500KB并在Jetson Nano上评测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 轻量端侧可部署异常检测，模型小于500KB并在Jetson Nano上评测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 轻量端侧可部署异常检测，模型小于500KB并在Jetson Nano上评测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: LMSAE is a lightweight multi-scale autoencoder for time-series anomaly detection on edge devices, using DWT features and under 500KB model size, achieving 9x latency and 2x power reduction on Jetson Nano with competitive accuracy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 物联网边缘场景下时序异常检测模型需在严格资源约束下运行，现有深度学习方法计算开销大且对细微异常不敏感。本文提出轻量多尺度自编码器LMSAE，利用离散小波变换提取多尺度特征并采用多尺度损失函数。实验表明，模型小于500KB，在Jetson Nano上推理延迟降低9倍、功耗降低2倍，同时保持竞争力检测精度，为端侧轻量安全检测提供了可参考的架构范式。

quick_skim.title: HeteroMosaic: Exposing and Exploiting Heterogeneous Execution Opportunities for Energy-Efficient Edge LLM Inference
quick_skim.abstract: Modern edge system-on-chips (SoCs) combine CPUs, integrated GPUs (iGPUs), and neural processing units (NPUs), yet existing LLM runtimes typically make coarse device-level decisions or optimize operators in isolation. As a result, they underutilize heterogeneous resources, particularly on unified-memory platforms where performance depends on both device placement and task-graph coordination.   We present HeteroMosaic, a heterogeneity-first scheduling framework for edge LLM inference. HeteroMosaic first uses a heterogeneous roofline model to identify when combining iGPU and NPU execution is beneficial. It then decomposes inference into dependency-preserving micro-batches that expose cross-accelerator overlap and applies trace-guided co-optimization of scheduling and device allocation under practical effects such as memory contention, DVFS, device variation, and NPU runtime overheads.   We implement HeteroMosaic in PyTorch C++ and evaluate it on three AMD Ryzen AI platforms spanning NPU-heavy, balanced, and iGPU-heavy designs. On the balanced platform, HeteroMosaic achieves up to 1.73X speedup over an iGPU baseline, 1.78X over an NPU baseline, and 2.05X over frameworks such as \texttt{llama.cpp}, while reducing energy by up to 45.3%. It also improves performance over prior heterogeneous edge AI solutions by up to 2.35X.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: energy-efficient edge LLM inference with heterogeneous scheduling on SoCs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: SoC上异构调度的能效边缘LLM推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: SoC上异构调度的能效边缘LLM推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: SoC上异构调度的能效边缘LLM推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Existing edge LLM runtimes underutilize heterogeneous SoC resources. Proposes HeteroMosaic, a heterogeneity-first scheduling framework that decomposes inference into micro-batches for cross-accelerator overlap, achieving up to 2.05X speedup and 45.3% energy reduction on AMD Ryzen AI platforms.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有边缘LLM运行时在异构SoC上资源利用率低，粗粒度设备决策导致性能不佳。本文提出HeteroMosaic异构优先调度框架，通过异构屋顶线模型识别协同执行机会，将推理分解为保持依赖的微批次以暴露跨加速器重叠，并在内存竞争等实际效应下协同优化调度与设备分配。在三个AMD Ryzen AI平台上的评估显示，最高实现2.05倍加速和45.3%能耗降低，为边缘LLM高效部署提供了重要基础。

quick_skim.title: Efficient and Privacy Aware Edge Cloud Collaborative Inference for Large Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: On-device LLM inference faces a trilemma of response latency, limited hardware resources and user privacy. Full cloud inference delivers strong computing power but exposes user prompts and dialogue data, while standalone on-device inference is unfeasible for most consumer and embedded edge devices. This paper presents a privacy-centric edge-cloud collaborative LLM inference framework built on endpoint-authenticated KV cache. Local endpoints handle input preprocessing, embedding computation, adaptive feature optimization, KV cache authentication, speculative decoding and low-dimensional model head calculation, while the cloud conducts authenticated decoder inference, KV cache management, token verification and high-dimensional vocabulary projection. Endpoints fuse partial outputs, apply language-adaptive masking and sample target tokens. All transmitted data and truncated logits are quantized and AES-GCM encrypted for privacy, with core lightweight modules, draft parameters and cache access policies kept local to avoid leakage. The framework supports heterogeneous devices including CPU-only, GPU-equipped and embedded devices via optimized streaming, batching and quantized ONNX deployment. Evaluations demonstrate that the framework reduces per-token latency by up to 46.1\% and downlink payloads by up to 67.4\% over baseline split inference, retaining comparable performance to full cloud inference.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: privacy-centric edge-cloud collaborative LLM inference with local lightweight modules and quantized ONNX deployment
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 隐私优先的边云协同LLM推理，本地轻量模块与量化ONNX部署
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 隐私优先的边云协同LLM推理，本地轻量模块与量化ONNX部署
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 隐私优先的边云协同LLM推理，本地轻量模块与量化ONNX部署
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: On-device LLM inference faces latency, resource, and privacy trilemma. This paper proposes a privacy-centric edge-cloud collaborative framework using endpoint-authenticated KV cache, keeping lightweight modules local while cloud handles heavy decoding, reducing per-token latency by 46.1% and downlink payloads by 67.4%.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 端侧LLM推理面临延迟、资源与隐私的三难困境。该文提出一种基于端点认证KV缓存的隐私优先边云协同推理框架，本地端点处理输入预处理、嵌入计算与推测解码等轻量任务，云端执行解码推理与高维投影，所有传输数据量化并AES-GCM加密。评估表明该框架相比基线分割推理降低46.1%的每token延迟和67.4%的下行负载，为隐私敏感的边缘LLM部署提供了实用方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 端侧LLM推理面临延迟、资源与隐私的三难困境。该文提出一种基于端点认证KV缓存的隐私优先边云协同推理框架，本地端点处理输入预处理、嵌入计算与推测解码等轻量任务，云端执行解码推理与高维投影，所有传输数据量化并AES-GCM加密。评估表明该框架相比基线分割推理降低46.1%的每token延迟和67.4%的下行负载，为隐私敏感的边缘LLM部署提供了实用方案。

quick_skim.title: Efficient and Privacy Aware Edge Cloud Collaborative Inference for Large Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: On-device LLM inference faces a trilemma of response latency, limited hardware resources and user privacy. Full cloud inference delivers strong computing power but exposes user prompts and dialogue data, while standalone on-device inference is unfeasible for most consumer and embedded edge devices. This paper presents a privacy-centric edge-cloud collaborative LLM inference framework built on endpoint-authenticated KV cache. Local endpoints handle input preprocessing, embedding computation, adaptive feature optimization, KV cache authentication, speculative decoding and low-dimensional model head calculation, while the cloud conducts authenticated decoder inference, KV cache management, token verification and high-dimensional vocabulary projection. Endpoints fuse partial outputs, apply language-adaptive masking and sample target tokens. All transmitted data and truncated logits are quantized and AES-GCM encrypted for privacy, with core lightweight modules, draft parameters and cache access policies kept local to avoid leakage. The framework supports heterogeneous devices including CPU-only, GPU-equipped and embedded devices via optimized streaming, batching and quantized ONNX deployment. Evaluations demonstrate that the framework reduces per-token latency by up to 46.1\% and downlink payloads by up to 67.4\% over baseline split inference, retaining comparable performance to full cloud inference.

quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有端侧LLM推理面临响应延迟、硬件资源受限与用户隐私的三难困境。该文提出基于端点认证KV缓存的隐私优先边缘云协同推理框架，本地端点处理轻量模块，云端执行重计算，支持量化ONNX部署。实验表明该框架相比基线分块推理降低46.1%每token延迟和67.4%下行负载，同时保持与全云推理相当的性能，为端侧安全部署提供可行路径。

quick_skim.title: DataShield: Uncovering Risky Fine-Tuning Data Across LLMs Through Consensus Subspace Alignment
quick_skim.abstract: Fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) on domain-specific datasets has become a standard paradigm for adapting LLMs to specialized applications. However, recent work has shown that even fine-tuning on benign task-specific data can substantially weaken the safety capabilities of LLMs. While existing efforts have made progress in identifying data responsible for safety degradation, they usually rely on a single mean vector computed over a specific model with its tokenizer to represent the safety direction, which limits both the effectiveness and transferability of their risk assessment measures. To address these limitations, we propose DataShield, a data assessment framework that identifies risky fine-tuning samples and response segments through consensus subspace alignment over joint safety-critical semantic spaces derived from multiple safety-aligned LLMs. Within these spaces, DataShield extracts consensus safe and unsafe subspaces using semantic spectral decomposition over safe and unsafe data representations. The risk of a data sample or segment is then estimated by measuring its relative alignment with the unsafe and safe subspaces, enabling both sample-level filtering and fine-grained segment-level masking. Compared with state-of-the-art filtering and masking baselines, DataShield reduces ASR by 14.6\% with sample filtering and 32.3\% with segment masking, while preserving downstream utility and avoiding target-model-specific risk computation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: subspace alignment for identifying risky fine-tuning data to preserve LLM safety
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于子空间对齐识别风险微调数据以保护LLM安全
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于子空间对齐识别风险微调数据以保护LLM安全
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于子空间对齐识别风险微调数据以保护LLM安全
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Fine-tuning on benign data can weaken LLM safety. DataShield identifies risky fine-tuning samples via consensus subspace alignment across multiple LLMs, reducing attack success rate by 14.6-32.3% while preserving utility.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 在良性数据上微调也会削弱LLM安全能力。现有方法依赖单一模型的安全方向表示，泛化性差。DataShield提出共识子空间对齐框架，从多个安全对齐LLM中提取安全与不安全子空间，通过衡量数据与子空间的对齐程度来识别风险样本和片段。实验表明，该方法在样本过滤和片段掩码上分别降低ASR 14.6%和32.3%，同时保持下游任务效用，为LLM安全微调提供了可迁移的数据审核方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 在良性数据上微调也会削弱LLM安全能力。现有方法依赖单一模型的安全方向表示，泛化性差。DataShield提出共识子空间对齐框架，从多个安全对齐LLM中提取安全与不安全子空间，通过衡量数据与子空间的对齐程度来识别风险样本和片段。实验表明，该方法在样本过滤和片段掩码上分别降低ASR 14.6%和32.3%，同时保持下游任务效用，为LLM安全微调提供了可迁移的数据审核方案。

quick_skim.title: ASK-NN: An Asymmetric Nearest-Neighbor Test that detects Distribution Drifts in Natural Language
quick_skim.abstract: Hallucinations and artificial text in LLM-generated outputs often appear as distributional deviations between prompt and response hidden-state distributions. Since prompts or retrieved contexts typically serve as reference samples and responses as query samples, with major differences in length, these asymmetries motivate the use of change test statistics that treat the two samples differently. We consider an asymmetric two-sample test ASK-NN based on the directed k-nearest-neighbor graph. Our statistic counts reference points whose nearest neighbor in the pooled sample is also a reference point. Under the permutation null, it admits an exact finite-sample conditional mean and variance; we further establish asymptotic normality and consistency under fixed alternatives. ASK-NN is computationally effective and easy to implement. Empirically, it is competitive with kernel and graph-based baselines on synthetic benchmarks, artificial-text detection, and LLM hallucination detection from token-level hidden states.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: training-free asymmetric nearest-neighbor test for detecting distribution drifts in LLM outputs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 无需训练的非对称最近邻检验用于检测LLM输出分布偏移
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 无需训练的非对称最近邻检验用于检测LLM输出分布偏移
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 无需训练的非对称最近邻检验用于检测LLM输出分布偏移
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: LLM hallucinations appear as distributional deviations between prompt and response hidden states. ASK-NN proposes a training-free asymmetric two-sample test using directed k-nearest-neighbor graphs, achieving competitive performance on hallucination and artificial-text detection.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: LLM幻觉常表现为提示与响应隐状态分布间的偏移。现有方法未充分考虑两者长度不对称性。ASK-NN提出一种基于有向k近邻图的无训练非对称两样本检验，在置换零假设下具有精确有限样本条件均值和方差。实验表明，该方法在人工文本检测和LLM幻觉检测上与核方法和图方法基线竞争力相当，为轻量级LLM输出安全检测提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: LLM幻觉常表现为提示与响应隐状态分布间的偏移。现有方法未充分考虑两者长度不对称性。ASK-NN提出一种基于有向k近邻图的无训练非对称两样本检验，在置换零假设下具有精确有限样本条件均值和方差。实验表明，该方法在人工文本检测和LLM幻觉检测上与核方法和图方法基线竞争力相当，为轻量级LLM输出安全检测提供了新思路。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: training-free lightweight guardrail for detecting unsafe LLM prompts and responses

quick_skim.quality_caution_reason_cn: 数据集或基准缺少可验证的公开证据
